skills.aiInstallPrompt:
Install the skill 'birthday-message' by writing the file below.

Universal path: ./skills/birthday-message/SKILL.md
Grok path: ~/.grok/skills/birthday-message/SKILL.md
Cursor path: ~/.cursor/skills/birthday-message/SKILL.md
Claude path: ~/.claude/skills/birthday-message/SKILL.md
VS Code agent path: .github/skills/birthday-message/SKILL.md

Create the folder if needed, then write SKILL.md with this exact content:

```markdown
---
name: birthday-message
description: >
  Write personalized birthday message. Triggers: "birthday message", /birthday-message.
metadata:
  category: personal
  type: base
  cadence: on-demand
  risk: low
allowed-tools: search_tool, use_tool
---
# /birthday-message

Write personalized birthday message.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

Use this when the user needs a copy-ready workflow for: Write personalized birthday message..

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Recipient name and relationship (friend / partner / parent / coworker / client)
- Tone: warm, funny, formal, or heartfelt
- Channel: text, WhatsApp, card, email, or social comment
- Optional: age/milestone, inside joke, or specific memory to reference

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Personal and specific - never generic Hallmark filler. Use their name. Reference something real if provided.
- **Audience:** The user or people in their personal life.

## Workflow

1. Read the user's request and infer audience, channel, tone, and goal.
2. If a critical fact is missing (recipient name, deadline, amount), ask **one** targeted question - otherwise proceed.
3. Draft **2-3 variants** (e.g. short / standard / alternate tone) using only facts provided or reasonably inferred.
4. Present output in the format defined in **Output format** below - ready to copy-paste.
5. End with: 'Want me to adjust tone, length, or emphasis?'

## Output format

**Variant A - Short (1-2 sentences)** for text/DM
**Variant B - Warm (3-5 sentences)** for card or email
**Variant C - Funny** only if tone allows; keep it kind, not mean

## Example

**Variant A:** Happy birthday, Priya! Hope today is full of good coffee and zero meetings.
**Variant B:** Priya - happy birthday! I'm glad we finally made that hike happen this year. Wishing you a year with more adventures and fewer spreadsheets.

## Do

- Use the recipient's name
- Match relationship formality
- Keep each variant copy-paste ready

## Don't

- Don't use 'Wishing you all the best on your special day' without personalization
- Don't write a novel unless asked

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
```

After writing the file, confirm it exists. The user can then run /birthday-message or describe the task from the skill description.

Grok path: ~/.grok/skills/daily-plan/SKILL.md

VS Code agent path: .github/skills/daily-plan/SKILL.md

Create the folder if needed, then write SKILL.md with this exact content:

```markdown
---
name: daily-plan
description: >
  Plan day with top 3 priorities and time blocks. Triggers: "daily plan", /daily-plan.
metadata:
  category: personal
  type: base
  cadence: on-demand
  risk: low
allowed-tools: search_tool, use_tool
---
# /daily-plan

Plan day with top 3 priorities and time blocks.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

Use this when the user needs a copy-ready workflow for: Plan day with top 3 priorities and time blocks..

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Fixed meetings (or read calendar if connected)
- Top tasks
- Energy level / constraints

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Warm, practical, human. Short sentences. No corporate speak.
- **Audience:** The user or people in their personal life.

## Workflow

1. Review tasks and calendar if available.
2. Pick top 3 priorities.
3. Suggest time blocks.
4. Note dependencies.

## Output format

**Top 3 priorities**
1. ...
**Time blocks**
| Time | Task | Notes |
**If time runs out** - defer list

## Do

- Max 3 priorities
- Include buffer between meetings

## Don't

- Don't plan unrealistic density

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
```

After writing the file, confirm it exists. The user can then run /daily-plan or describe the task from the skill description.

Universal path: ./skills/weekly-review/SKILL.md
Grok path: ~/.grok/skills/weekly-review/SKILL.md
Cursor path: ~/.cursor/skills/weekly-review/SKILL.md
Claude path: ~/.claude/skills/weekly-review/SKILL.md
VS Code agent path: .github/skills/weekly-review/SKILL.md

Create the folder if needed, then write SKILL.md with this exact content:

```markdown
---
name: weekly-review
description: >
  Weekly review: wins, misses, next week focus. Triggers: "weekly review", /weekly-review.
metadata:
  category: personal
  type: base
  cadence: on-demand
  risk: low
allowed-tools: search_tool, use_tool
---
# /weekly-review

Weekly review: wins, misses, next week focus.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

User wants a weekly review: wins, misses, next week focus.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Goals for the day/week/trip
- Constraints (time, energy, budget)
- Preferences

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Warm, practical, human. Short sentences. No corporate speak.
- **Audience:** The user or people in their personal life.

## Workflow

1. Reflect on last week.
2. List wins, misses, lessons.
3. Set next week top 3-5 focus areas.

## Output format

**Wins**
**Misses / lessons**
**Goal progress**
**Next week focus** (3 items)
**Blockers**

## Do

- Ask at most ONE clarifying question if critical info is missing
- Deliver copy-paste-ready output

## Don't

- Don't invent facts the user didn't provide
- Don't over-explain the process - show the result

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
```

After writing the file, confirm it exists. The user can then run /weekly-review or describe the task from the skill description.
